howItWorks.intro: This endurance vo2 max predictor uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: VO2 Max (Daniels/Gilbert)
rules.explanation: Uses the Daniels and Gilbert running economy model to estimate aerobic capacity from race performance.

rules.name: Pace
rules.formula: Pace = Total Time / Distance in Miles
rules.explanation: Average pace per mile during the race.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.q: What is a good VO2 max?
faq.a: For males, 40-50 ml/kg/min is average to good, 50-60 is excellent, and 60+ is elite. For females, 35-42 is average to good and 50+ is elite. VO2 max declines about 10% per decade after age 30, but training slows this significantly.

faq.q: How accurate is VO2 max from race times?
faq.a: Race time estimates are within 5-10% of lab-tested values for trained runners. The prediction works best for 5K-10K races. Very short (1 mile) or very long (marathon) races may be less accurate due to anaerobic and fueling factors respectively.

howWeCalculate.intro: This endurance vo2 max predictor uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above
